name: CFOP (Fridrich)
description: The most popular speedcubing method, used by the majority of world-class solvers. CFOP stands for Cross, F2L, OLL, PLL — four distinct stages that break the solve into manageable pieces.

steps.name: Cross
steps.description: Solve the 4 edge pieces of one face (usually white) to form a plus sign on the bottom layer, ensuring edges align with center colors.
tips: Plan the entire cross during inspection
Solve on the bottom to see F2L pairs

steps.name: F2L (First Two Layers)
steps.description: Pair corner and edge pieces together and insert them into their correct slots, solving the first two layers simultaneously.
tips: Reduce rotations by inserting from different angles

steps.name: OLL (Orientation of Last Layer)
steps.description: Orient all pieces on the last layer so the top face is one solid color (yellow if white cross).

steps.name: PLL (Permutation of Last Layer)
steps.description: Permute all last layer pieces to their correct positions, completing the solve.

cons: F2L can be difficult to learn intuitively
creator: Jessica Fridrich
